tool.name: recetox-xMSannotator: Reformat Annotation
tool.description: reformat annotation table and correlation matrix for chemscore computation

configfiles.configfile:
# Load input tables annotation <- load_table("$annotation", "$annotation.ext") peak_table <- load_table("$peak_table", "$peak_table.ext") peak_correlation_matrix <- load_table("$peak_correlation_matrix", "$peak_correlation_matrix.ext")
# Reformat annotation table for chemscore computation annotation <- recetox.xmsannotator::reformat_annotation_table(annotation)
# Reformat correlation matrix to global correlation format global_cor <- recetox.xmsannotator::reformat_correlation_matrix( peak_table, peak_correlation_matrix )
# Save outputs save_table(annotation, "$annotation_output", "$annotation_output.ext") save_table(global_cor, "$global_cor_output", "$global_cor_output.ext")

param.name: annotation
param.label: Annotation table
param.help: Annotation table from Compute Isotopes tool. Contains compound annotations with isotope information.

param.name: peak_table
param.label: Peak table
param.help: Peak table with intensities, modules, RT clusters, and mass defect. Used to compute the global correlation output.

param.name: peak_correlation_matrix
param.label: Peak correlation matrix
param.help: Correlation matrix from Compute Peak Correlations tool. Will be reformatted for downstream chemscore computation.

tool.help:
**Reformat Annotation**
Prepare annotation table and correlation matrix for chemscore computation.
Description ===========
This tool performs essential data reformatting required before computing chemical scores (chemscores). It transforms the annotation table and correlation matrix into formats compatible with the chemscore algorithm.
What This Tool Does -------------------
### 1. Reformat Annotation Table
The `reformat_annotation_table` function: - Restructures annotation columns for chemscore input - Ensures proper data types for scoring calculations - Prepares isotope group information for scoring
### 2. Reformat Correlation Matrix
The `reformat_correlation_matrix` function: - Converts the full correlation matrix to a "global correlation" format - Computes summary statistics needed for scoring - Creates a lookup structure for efficient correlation queries during scoring
Inputs ------
**Annotation Table** Output from Compute Isotopes tool containing: - Compound annotations - Isotope assignments (ISgroup) - Adduct information - Mass and retention time data
**Peak Table** Full peak table with: - Peak identifiers - Intensities across samples - Module assignments - RT cluster assignments - Mass defect values
**Peak Correlation Matrix** The correlation matrix computed earlier in the pipeline.
Outputs -------
**Reformatted Annotation** Annotation table reformatted for chemscore computation. Column structure may differ from input to match chemscore expectations.
**Global Correlation** Reformatted correlation data optimized for fast lookups during chemscore calculation.
Usage Notes -----------
This is a bridge tool between isotope detection and chemscore computation. Both outputs are required by the Compute Chemscore tool.
Typical Workflow Position -------------------------
Compute Isotopes → Reformat Annotation → Compute Chemscore
The reformatted annotation becomes the primary input for chemscore, while the global correlation provides context for how each peak relates to all others.
Technical Details -----------------
The reformatted annotation typically includes: - One row per unique chemical_ID - Aggregated isotope/adduct information - Pre-computed statistics for scoring
The global correlation output enables the chemscore algorithm to efficiently query: - How well does this peak correlate with other peaks in its module? - What is the overall correlation structure of the dataset?
References ----------
Novotný, J., et al. (2024). recetox-xMSannotator: Advanced metabolite annotation tool. Analytical Chemistry. DOI: 10.1021/acs.analchem.6b01214
